title: Lastrea hallieri
taxonomy: Lastrea hallieri is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is part of the family Dryopteridaceae. The genus Lastrea is a group of ferns that are commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. These ferns are known for their delicate and lacy fronds, which are often used in traditional medicine and as ornamental plants. They are also important components of their ecosystems, providing habitat and food for various animals and insects.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Some studies suggest that Lastrea hallieri may also have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in fighting off infections and promoting wound healing.
